products.name: EPiC Propulsion Battery
products.descriptor: Modular lithium-ion propulsion battery ecosystem for electric aircraft

evidence.snippet: EPiC Propulsion Battery

In 2021, EP Systems released EPiC 1.0. It addressed some of the most prevalent roadblocks to advanced air mobility – including safety, cost, weight, and infrastructure. This lightweight, high-power system offers an impressive 60 minutes of usable flight time with 30 minutes of reserve.

products.name: Battery Power Management Unit (BPMU)
products.descriptor: Battery management unit with isolation detection, charging self-protection, and system status monitoring

evidence.snippet:
![Black BPMU Rendering](https://epsenergy.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/Black-BPMU-Rendering-300x300.png "Black BPMU Rendering")
Battery Power Management Unit

The BPMU monitors and reports system status, communicating with each component in the system and enacting controls as directed by the operator. Multiple safety features have been included in the design of the BPMU, including Isolation Detection, system charging self-protection, and numerous status warnings and messages relating to safe operation of high voltage propulsion batteries.

products.name: Midpoint Disconnect (MPD)
products.descriptor: High-voltage contactor and fuse assembly for safe battery string isolation and maintenance

evidence.snippet:
![Midpoint Disconect](https://epsenergy.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/midpoint-discconect-hires_02.png "Midpoint Disconect")
Midpoint Disconnect
The MPD contains an electrical contactor and the system high voltage fuse. The fuse may be manually removed to reduce effective battery string voltage for safe handling and maintenance operations.

products.name: Battery Module
products.descriptor: Cell-level monitoring module with voltage/temperature sensing and passive balancing for EPiC battery strings

evidence.snippet:
![EPiC Energy battery](https://epsenergy.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/EPiC-Energy-battery-square-300x300.png "EPiC Energy battery")
Battery Module
Each Battery Module monitors cell voltage and temperature, and enacts passive balancing to ensure that string voltage remains balanced and optimized.

products.name: DC Fast Charger (200 kW)
products.descriptor: Proprietary 200 kW DC fast charger for rapid electric aircraft turnaround

evidence.snippet:
### DC Fast Charging
EP Systems is developing a proprietary 200 kW DC Fast Charger to enable rapid charging and turn around times of electric aircraft.

products.name: Overnight Wall Charger (20 kW)
products.descriptor: 20 kW hangar wall charger for slow overnight charging and battery cycle life optimization

evidence.snippet:
### Overnight Wall Charger
The 20 kW Wall Charger will allow for slower charging in the hangar, and optimize battery state of charge levels for extended cycle and calendar life of the system.

products.name: Mobile Microgrid Charge Truck
products.descriptor: Mobile or stationary second-life battery microgrid charging solution for electric aircraft at airports

evidence.snippet:
### Mobile Microgrid Charge Truck
The Mobile Micro Grid charging solution provides a secondary life for batteries, reducing overall costs and maximizing efficient use of energy and materials. The Micro Grid may be installed as a stationary system directly at airports, or used in a mobile application for a more flexible charging solution.

products.name: Custom Solution
products.descriptor: Custom-engineered battery thermal containment and propulsion energy systems for diverse electric mobility applications

evidence.snippet: Custom Solution

### EP Systems Thermal Containment: Over 50 Proven Systems
EP Systems has developed a wide variety of custom systems for many electric propulsion mobility applications. A significant design consideration for lithium ion batteries is safe mitigation of fire, or thermal runaway events. EP Systems has designed multiple systems that mitigate this risk in various ways.

unresolved_questions: EPiC 2.0 readiness marked unknown: corpus states it was 'undergoing safety of flight development' scheduled for early 2025; no explicit production confirmation found in fetched pages.
DC Fast Charger (200 kW) marked prototype: corpus explicitly states EP Systems 'is developing' it; actual production status cannot be confirmed from corpus.
VersaPower, Power Distribution Unit (PMU/HV/LV), Overnight Wall Charger, and Mobile Microgrid Charge Truck readiness marked unknown: no maturity language found for these products in corpus.
No NDAA Section 848/889 or Blue UAS claims found for any product in the fetched corpus.
Defense page (epsenergy.com/defense) and Commercial page (epsenergy.com/commercial) were skipped; they may contain additional product details or compliance claims not captured here.
